chapter.title: Overview
chapter.simpara: The Elasticsearch plugin connects QALIPSIS to Elasticsearch.

listitem.simpara: Elasticsearch: https://www.elastic.co/
JAVA API: refer to Elasticsearch JAVA Client.
poll: polls the newest data periodically.
save: saves a batch records generated from the input and step context.
search: searches records and builds a query from the input and step context.

chapter.simpara: Example scenarios using a combination of Elasticsearch supported steps are provided on Github.

chapter.title: Poll step
chapter.simpara: The poll step within the Elasticsearch plugin periodically polls data from Elasticsearch. The output is a list of ElasticsearchDocument containing JSON data or deserialized to objects.

listitem.simpara: Scenario

The poll step is created only once in a scenario; it is then used throughout the scenario as called. The poll step within the Elasticsearch plugin uses a strategy of “delivered exactly once”. The output of the poll step is an ElasticsearchDocument that contains maps of column to values.
index: sets the elasticsearch indices to use for the queries; defaults to all.
client: configures the REST client to connect to Elasticsearch.
query: configures the builder for the JSON query to perform the first poll.
queryParameters(optional): adds parameters to the query.
pollDelay (required): specifies the delay between query executions.
broadcast (optional): specifies the broadcast parameters for the step.
The poll step is generally used in conjunction with a join to assert data or inject data into a workflow.
The query parameter requires at least one sorting field to filter the newest results and not fetch the same records repeatedly.

The output of the poll step is an ElasticsearchDocument that contains maps of column names to values. If flatten is called, the records are provided one by one to the next step; otherwise each poll batch remains complete.
Refer to Elasticsearch Query Parameters for more information on query parameters.
Refer to the Elasticsearch documentation for further parameter and configuration information.

chapter.title: Save step
chapter.simpara: The save step within the Elasticsearch plugin persists a batch of documents into Elasticsearch.

listitem.simpara: Step
The save step’s input and step context are used to generate a list of documents that are forwarded to Elasticsearch.
client: configures the REST client to connect to Elasticsearch.
documents: defines the strategy to create documents to save from the input and step context.
Refer to the Elasticsearch documentation for further parameter information.

chapter.title: Search step
chapter.simpara: The search step within the Elasticsearch plugin searches documents in the Elasticsearch database using a search query.

listitem.simpara: Step
The search step’s input and step context are used to generate a query; the batch of results is then forwarded to the next step(s).
client: configures the REST client to connect to Elasticsearch.
index: sets the elasticsearch indices to use for the queries; defaults to all.
query: configures the builder for the JSON query to perform a search.
Refer to the Elasticsearch documentation for further parameter and configuration information.
For specifics on queryParameters refer to Elasticsearch Query Parameters.
For specifics on search parameters, refer to Elasticsearch Search Parameters.

chapter.title: Configuration of publication of events and meters to Elasticsearch

listitem.simpara: enabled: boolean flag that activates/deactivates event publishing to Elasticsearch; defaults to false; must be set to true.
min-level: minimal accepted level of events; defaults to INFO; allowable values are `EventLevel`values: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, OFF.
urls: list of URLs to the Elasticsearch instances; defaults to http://localhost:9200.
path-prefix: string prefix used to demarcate url paths; defaults to /.
index-prefix: string prefix to use for the created indices containing the events; defaults to qalipsis-events.
refresh-interval: string delay to refresh the indices in Elasticsearch; defaults to 10s.
store-source: boolean flag identifies whether the source of documents is stored with the values; set to true for yes and false for no; defaults to false.
index-date-pattern: string format of the date part of the index as supported by [java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter]; defaults to yyyy-MM-dd.
linger-period: maximum period between publication of events to Elasticsearch; positive duration, defaults to 10s.
batch-size: maximum number of events buffered between two publications of events to Elasticsearch; positive integer; defaults to 2000.
publishers: number of concurrent publication of events that can run; positive integer; defaults to 1 (no concurrency).
username: name of the user for basic authentication when connecting to Elasticsearch.
password: password of the user for basic authentication when connecting to Elasticsearch.
shards: number of shards to apply on the created indices for events; positive integer; defaults to 1.
replicas: number of replicas to apply on the created indices for events; defaults to 0.
proxy: URL of the http proxy to use to access Elasticsearch.
enabled: boolean flag that activates/deactivates meter publishing to Elasticsearch; defaults to false; must be set to true.
step(optional): The step size (reporting frequency) to use; defaults to PT10S.
urls: list of urls to the Elasticsearch instances; defaults to http://localhost:9200.
host: defaults to the value of urls above.
index: string value to use for creation of index containing the meters; defaults to qalipsis-meters.
index-date-format: string format of the date part of the index as supported by [java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter]; defaults to yyyy-MM-dd.
auto-create-index: boolean flag to enable/disable auto creation of index; defaults to true.

section.title: Example
section.programlisting:
monitoring: elasticsearch: hosts: http://localhost:9200 enabled: true step: PT1M index: qalipsis-metrics index-date-format: yyyy-MM-dd auto-create-index: true
events: export: elasticsearch: enabled: true min-level: INFO urls: http://localhost:9200 index-prefix: qalipsis-events index-date-pattern: yyyy-MM-dd duration-as-nano: false linger-period: 10s batch-size: 2000 publishers: 1 username: password: shards: 1 replicas: 0
meters: export: elasticsearch: urls: http://localhost:9200 host: ${meters.export.elasticsearch.urls} enabled: true step: PT10S index: qalipsis-meters index-date-format: yyyy-MM-dd auto-create-index: true
